title: The five-minute evening download
subtitle: A short closing ritual for emptying the mind before sleep. Three things done, one thing set aside, one thing to look forward to.
excerpt: A simple five-minute bedtime ritual from Day 6 of the SLEEP program. Three prompts, five minutes, a clean close to the day. Not journalling, a release valve.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A five-minute closing ritual for Day 6 of the SLEEP program. Three prompts, written by hand or spoken aloud, that give the mind a structured end point. Five minutes, the same time each night.
A notebook or scrap of paper
Five minutes before you put your phone down
Each night, write three short answers, one to each prompt. No full sentences required. A word or a phrase is enough.
Not necessarily good things. Just what happened. This empties the holding pattern (the mind replaying the day trying to make sense of it) by giving it a container.
One thing that is still sitting with me
Name it. Don't try to resolve it, just acknowledge it. Write: "Not tonight." beside it. Tomorrow is for this.
One thing I am looking forward to tomorrow
Anything. Coffee. A task. A conversation. Something to give the mind a forward anchor that isn't anxious.
Close the notebook. The day is done.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
